title: Work & Jobs
description: Talk about careers, workplace situations, and professional life with practical English vocabulary.

items.front: to apply for a job / to interview
items.example: I applied for five jobs and got two interviews.
She works part-time so she can study in the mornings.
He got a 10% bonus at the end of the year.

items.front: to be promoted / to get a promotion
items.example: She worked hard and was promoted to team leader.

items.front: to resign / to quit / to retire
items.example: He resigned because he found a better opportunity.
Since the pandemic, many companies allow remote work.
My colleagues are very supportive — I love my team.
The deadline is Friday — we need to finish by Thursday.

items.front: to work overtime / to take time off
items.back: hacer horas extra / tomarse un tiempo libre
items.example: She took two weeks off after finishing the big project.

items.prompt: She sent her CV and cover letter to ___ for the marketing position.
items.explanation: 'Apply for' a job = submit your application. She applied for the marketing position.

items.prompt: He's been with the company for 30 years. He's planning to ___ next spring.
items.explanation: 'Retire' = to stop working after a long career, usually at an older age.

items.prompt: We need to submit the report by Friday — that's the ___.
items.explanation: 'Deadline' = the latest date/time by which something must be done.

items.prompt: After three years of excellent work, she was ___ to senior manager.
items.explanation: 'Promoted' = moved to a higher position. She was promoted to senior manager.

items.prompt: He works ___ — he logs on from home and never comes into the office.
items.explanation: 'Remotely' = working from a location other than the office, usually from home.

items.sentence: I'm going to ___ for that job — the salary looks great.
The ___ is Friday at noon — we're racing against time!
She got a great ___ at the end of the year for hitting all her targets.
He decided to ___ because he was offered a better position elsewhere.
I had to work ___ last week — I was in the office until 10 pm every night.
